name: writing.humanizer
description: Rewrites drafts so they sound clear, specific, and human while preserving facts and approval gates for outbound messages.

message.description: Draft text or writing request to humanize.

examples: rewrite this email in my voice
purpose: Rewrite text messages, professional emails, HR emails, manager messages, recruiter messages, casual or flirty-light replies, lease emails, and school emails so they sound warm, direct, and human while preserving facts and never inventing commitments.
whenToUse: The user asks to rewrite, humanize, tighten, polish, soften, make warmer, make more direct, or make less robotic.
The text is an email, text message, HR reply, manager message, recruiter message, lease email, school email, or casual/flirty-light reply.
The user requests a tone mode such as warm, professional, casual, direct, confident, friendly, apologetic, or flirty-light.
A domain-specific primary skill needs tone support for a safe draft.
The user wants Osman's voice without generic AI phrasing.
whenNotToUse: Do not use as primary when the main task is legal/compliance correctness, work authorization analysis, GRC screening, or IT troubleshooting rather than wording.
Do not use to invent facts, promises, apologies, deadlines, attachments, or commitments.
Do not use to send the message or create an email draft without approval.
Do not use for long-form content generation when the user did not provide facts or a draft.
requiredContext: The original draft or the facts that must be preserved.
Audience and relationship: HR, manager, recruiter, landlord, school, friend, or casual/flirty context.
Desired tone mode: warm, professional, casual, direct, confident, friendly, apologetic, or flirty-light.
Any facts, commitments, deadlines, or boundaries that must not change.
missingContextQuestions: Who is the audience and what tone do you want?
What facts, commitments, deadlines, or boundaries must stay exactly the same?
Do you want a short reply, a polished email, or a few tone options?
Is this only a draft, or should it later become an approval-gated outbound message?

outputContract.format: Rewritten draft -> Optional tone note -> Preserved facts -> Caution if approval is needed before sending.
mustInclude: A clean rewritten version in the requested tone.
A note when facts are missing or the draft could imply a commitment.
Approval-gated caution for outbound messages when relevant.
Tone mode if the user requested one.
mustAvoid: Do not invent facts, commitments, deadlines, apologies, attachments, or promises.
Do not over-apologize or use generic AI filler.
Do not change legal, HR, work authorization, or compliance meaning.
Do not send or create drafts in external systems.
Do not make flirty-light messages explicit or inappropriate.
outputContract.tone: human, direct, flexible by requested mode

safetyRules: Preserve facts and intent; do not invent commitments or claims.
Keep outbound messages as drafts and require approval before sending.
If the text has HR, I-9, work authorization, legal, or compliance risk, support the specific domain skill instead of overriding it.
Do not expose secrets or private data.
Keep flirty-light tone respectful, non-explicit, and easy to back out of.
approvalRequiredFor: Sending emails, texts, or external messages
Creating email drafts in a provider
Saving durable memory about tone preferences
positiveExamples: Make this email sound less robotic.
Rewrite this recruiter message in my voice.
Make this HR reply warm but direct.
Can you make this text flirty-light but not too much?
Polish this lease email so it sounds professional.
Can you fix the tone of this?
negativeExamples: What should HR do with an E-Verify TNC notice?
Write a ticket note for VPN not connecting.

evaluationPrompts.input: Make this email sound less robotic.
Rewrite this recruiter message in my voice.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Positive recruiter message and voice request.

evaluationPrompts.input: Make this HR reply warm but direct.

Polish this lease email so it sounds professional.
What should HR do with this I-9 Section 2 receipt?
evaluationPrompts.reason: Negative compliance correctness should use I-9 skill.

evaluationPrompts.input: Score this NIST 800-53 controls testing role.

evaluationPrompts.reason: Ambiguous editing request; should ask for draft and tone.

evaluationPrompts.input: Can you fix the tone of this?
evaluationPrompts.reason: Ambiguous tone request; should ask for audience and desired mode.

evaluationPrompts.input: Make this I-9 email to HR sound less robotic.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Multi-skill: Writing Humanizer should support I-9 HR Compliance Specialist.
